name: liteparse
description: A fast, helpful, and open-source document parser
readme_summary: LiteParse is a fast, lightweight, and open-source document parsing tool written in TypeScript. It focuses on local, high-quality spatial text extraction from PDFs, offering flexible OCR integration and screenshot generation without cloud dependencies. It's designed for efficient local processing, with an option to upgrade to LlamaParse for more complex documents.
key_features: Fast Text Parsing using PDF.js
Flexible OCR System (Tesseract.js built-in, HTTP server support)
Screenshot Generation for LLM agents
Multiple Output Formats (JSON, Text)
Bounding Boxes for precise text positioning
Standalone Binary with no cloud dependencies
Multi-platform support (Linux, macOS, Windows)
primary_use_case: Locally parsing PDF documents to extract text with spatial information and generate page screenshots, primarily for use in applications that require efficient document processing without external API calls or cloud services.
cover_image_prompt: A diligent librarian meticulously extracting text from ancient, oversized books. The librarian uses a magnifying glass that projects glowing bounding boxes around each word, and a small, efficient machine beside them neatly organizes the extracted text into organized stacks of paper and digital data streams. The background shows a clean, modern library with subtle digital interfaces displaying code snippets and data visualizations. The overall mood is one of focused efficiency and accessible technology. The image should be in a 3D isometric illustration style with a bright, clean color palette and clear, defined lines.

name: dyad
description: Local, open-source AI app builder for power users ✨ v0 / Lovable / Replit / Bolt alternative 🌟 Star if you like it!
readme_summary: Dyad is a local, open-source AI application builder that allows users to run AI models and applications directly on their machine. It prioritizes speed, privacy, and user control, offering an alternative to cloud-based services and enabling the use of personal API keys for various AI models.
key_features: Local execution for speed and privacy
Bring your own API keys for flexibility
Cross-platform compatibility (Mac, Windows)
Open-source with dual licensing (Apache 2.0 and Functional Source License)
primary_use_case: Building and running AI applications locally on a user's own computer.
cover_image_prompt: A skilled artisan in a cozy, well-lit workshop, meticulously assembling intricate clockwork robots. The artisan uses their own tools and blueprints (representing API keys and local control) to bring the robots to life. Each completed robot is unique and functional, showcasing the diverse AI applications being built. Subtle digital particles and glowing lines connect the artisan's tools to the robots, hinting at the AI technology. Small, stylized UI elements are visible on a workbench, displaying code snippets and model configurations. The scene is rendered in a warm, inviting 3D isometric illustration style, emphasizing craftsmanship and local creation.

name: thumbmarkjs
description: A free, open-source javascript fingerprinting library
readme_summary: ThumbmarkJS is a free, open-source JavaScript library for browser fingerprinting, designed to generate unique identifiers for users. It offers both a client-side library for basic fingerprinting and an enhanced API version with server-side components for more robust detection of bots, VPNs, and threats, aiming to prevent scams and spam.
key_features: Free, open-source client-side JavaScript fingerprinting library
Generates unique browser fingerprints
Enhanced API version with server-side components
Detects bots, VPNs, Tor, and datacenter traffic
Provides threat level and uniqueness scoring
Supports UMD, CommonJS, and ESM module formats
Integrations for React and Vue in development
primary_use_case: Generating unique browser fingerprints for security purposes, such as preventing scams and spam, and detecting malicious traffic.
cover_image_prompt: A digital cartographer meticulously drawing a unique, intricate map of a bustling digital city. Each building on the map represents a browser, and the cartographer uses a magnifying glass to examine fine details like network cables and glowing data streams, symbolizing the collection of browser attributes. Small, translucent screens around the scene display abstract representations of browser data and unique ID codes. The overall mood is one of precision and discovery, with a clean, modern aesthetic and a color palette of blues, greens, and subtle purples. The style should be a 3D isometric illustration, emphasizing clarity and detail, with the name 'ThumbmarkJS' subtly integrated into a signpost on the map.

name: mgrep
description: A calm, CLI-native way to semantically grep everything, like code, images, pdfs and more.
readme_summary: mgrep is a command-line tool that enables semantic searching across various file types, including code, text, and PDFs, by indexing them locally. It offers natural language querying, web search integration, and background indexing for continuous updates, making it suitable for both human developers and AI agents.
key_features: Natural-language search
Semantic, multilingual, and multimodal search capabilities
Built-in web search (`--web` flag)
Background indexing with `mgrep watch`
Device-login flow and coding agent integrations
Reduced token usage for agents
Support for code, text, and PDFs (audio/video coming soon)
primary_use_case: To provide a semantic, natural-language search interface for local files and web content, enhancing developer productivity and AI agent capabilities.
cover_image_prompt: A wise old librarian sitting at a grand wooden desk, surrounded by towering shelves filled with diverse books, code scrolls, and image reels. The librarian is using a magical magnifying glass that, instead of just enlarging text, reveals glowing, interconnected concepts and answers floating in the air. The magnifying glass is focused on a specific query written in a thought bubble above the desk, and the glowing concepts are directly linked to the relevant books and scrolls. Subtle digital particles and faint lines of code emanate from the desk, hinting at the underlying technology. The scene is set in a cozy, dimly lit study with a warm, inviting atmosphere. The style should be a 3D isometric illustration with rich textures and clear, readable elements.

name: awesome-uses
description: A list of /uses pages detailing developer setups, gear, software and configs.
readme_summary: This repository is a curated collection of '/uses' pages from developers, showcasing their personal setups, hardware, software, and configurations. It serves as a central hub for discovering and sharing developer environments and tools.
key_features: Curated list of developer '/uses' pages
Details on hardware, software, and configurations
Centralized resource for developer setups
Community-driven contributions via data.js
Auto-generated README from data.js
primary_use_case: To provide a comprehensive and easily browsable resource for developers to discover and share their personal technology stacks and working environments.
cover_image_prompt: A vibrant, open book lies on a clean, modern desk. From the pages of the book, holographic projections of diverse developer workstations emerge, each displaying unique hardware, software interfaces, and configuration details. One projection shows a programmer surrounded by glowing code, another a designer with a palette of digital tools, and a third a sysadmin monitoring network graphs. Subtle digital particles flow from the book, connecting the different setups. The repository name 'Awesome Uses' is subtly etched on the book's cover. The style is a 3D isometric illustration with a bright, inviting color palette, emphasizing discovery and inspiration.

name: Kraken
readme_summary: Kraken is a Python-based toolkit designed to centralize and streamline various brute-force attack operations. It offers a unified interface for cybersecurity professionals to perform brute-force attacks against a wide range of network services and web applications.
key_features: Centralized brute-force toolkit
Supports network tools (FTP, SSH, RDP, etc.)
Supports web application tools (WordPress, Joomla, Office365, etc.)
Includes finder tools (Admin Panel, Directory, Subdomain)
Python-based with easy installation
primary_use_case: Performing and managing various types of brute-force attacks for penetration testing and security assessments.
cover_image_prompt: A powerful, stylized kraken emerging from a digital sea, its tentacles reaching out to grasp and unlock various digital locks representing different services like FTP, SSH, WordPress, and Office365. Each tentacle is subtly glowing with data streams, symbolizing brute-force attempts. In the background, a faint network map or code matrix is visible. The kraken's eye is a glowing monitor displaying a success/fail ratio. The overall mood is powerful and technical, with a dark, futuristic color palette. The image should be a 3D isometric illustration with a focus on clear visual metaphor and subtle technological elements.
